refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety compatibility criteria, such as p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation
Design a real-time data compression and prioritization mechanism to minimize transmission delays
Establish a distributed control architecture with local autonomy to reduce dependency on end-to-end latency
Incorporate predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Use a modular communication interface that can adapt to varying network conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation

potential_sources: Dependency between communication protocol and control algorithms
Interaction between data compression and latency requirements
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on the communication protocol layer, which can be designed independently from control algorithms and other system components, supporting modularity and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Predictive control or local autonomy is not required to meet latency requirements
Communication protocol can be optimized without impacting other system functions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive mechanisms or local autonomy in the DP if network variability or latency spikes are expected beyond the agreed conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for enabling remote control and interaction with the vehicle, including communication protocols for teleoperation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for vehicle state and control feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control instructions from remote operator

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection for communication

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many unrelated functions, leading to high diffusion and low cohesion for this DP.
Vehicle module focuses on physical vehicle systems; communication protocol for teleoperation is only partially contained and would diffuse heavily outside.
Highly cohesive with the DP; communication protocol stack is central to teleoperation functionality with minimal diffusion.
Safety services focus on safety alerts and monitoring; communication protocol stack is not well contained here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing the dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface responsibilities if physical layer details are not clearly separated.
If teleoperation functionality expands, the communication protocol stack might need to interface with Safety Services, increasing coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a dedicated low-latency communication protocol stack optimized for teleoperation, which is necessary to achieve the required end-to-end latency compatible with safe remote operation (p95 ≤ 150 ms). This mapping is clear and focused on the functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface responsibilities if physical layer details are not clearly separated.
If teleoperation functionality expands, the communication protocol stack might need to interface with Safety Services, increasing coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2) for the DP, indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion, confirming Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by testing the Teleoperation module's communication latency under agreed network conditions to ensure p95 ≤ 150 ms.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation IM boundary is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces defined for Telemetry, Tasking, and Network Link supporting the implementation and verification of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the separation of responsibilities between the Teleoperation module and the Network Link interface to avoid physical layer overlap.
Plan for potential future integration points with Safety Services to manage coupling if teleoperation functionality expands.
Consider defining interface contracts explicitly to maintain low diffusion and high cohesion as the system evolves.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear, focused mappings and strong containment of the Teleoperation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are established, and the DP directly addresses the FR with no immediate need for further decomposition. Recommended revisions pertain to documentation and interface clarity rather than functional decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and well-contained module is preferable to avoid complexity.

revise.why: Clarify and document separation of responsibilities between Teleoperation module and Network Link interface to avoid physical layer overlap; plan for future integration with Safety Services; define explicit interface contracts to maintain low diffusion and high cohesion.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR with the specified latency requirements.
The Teleoperation module's high containment and low diffusion scores indicate a robust architectural boundary suitable for implementation.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Network Link interface responsibilities if physical layer details are not clearly separated.
Increased coupling risk if teleoperation functionality expands to interface with Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between Teleoperation module and Network Link interface.
Verification test results demonstrating communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions.
